com.testingtech.tworkbench.ttman.server.api.impl.job

Class JobImpl

  • java.lang.Object
    • com.testingtech.tworkbench.ttman.server.api.impl.job.JobImpl
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    Job
    Direct Known Subclasses:
    BatchExecuteJobImpl, ExecuteTestCaseJobImpl

    public abstract class JobImpl
extends java.lang.Object
implements Job
    A handler for the asynchronous test execution. A job is created when IExecutionServer.executeControl(String) or IExecutionServer.executeTestCase(String, String, java.util.List) was called. The jobs are then enqueued are are processed by the server. At a given time, only one execute test case job can be active for a given client. The job's life cycle is defined by it's state see JobStatus for details.
    Since:
    TTworkbench 15

        public Job getParentJob()
        Description copied from interface: Job
        If this job was started by another job (e.g. control execution job), the starter-job is the parent.
        Specified by:
        getParentJob in interface Job
        Returns:
        the parent job which started this job

                      boolean notify)
        Method should not be called by client since sets the new job status

        notify - true to notify listeners that state has changed

        Notify all listeners that the status has changed

      • finalize

        protected void finalize()
                 throws java.lang.Throwable
        Removes the job from the JobRegistry, and then calls Object.finalize().
        Overrides:
        finalize in class java.lang.Object
        Throws:
        java.lang.Throwable

      • release

        public void release()
        Removes the job from the JobRegistry.

      • join

        public void join()
          throws java.lang.InterruptedException
        Description copied from interface: Job
        Block until job execution finishes (status is JobStatus.CANCELLED or JobStatus.FINISHED).
        Specified by:
        join in interface Job
        Throws:
        java.lang.InterruptedException - if any thread interrupted the current thread before or while the current thread was waiting for job execution to finish. The interrupted status of the current thread is cleared when this exception is thrown.

      • join

        public void join(long milliseconds)
          throws java.lang.InterruptedException,
                 java.util.concurrent.TimeoutException
        Description copied from interface: Job
        Block until job execution finishes or timeout (status is JobStatus.CANCELLED or JobStatus.FINISHED).
        Specified by:
        join in interface Job
        Parameters:
        milliseconds - timeout for wait in milliseconds
        Throws:
        java.lang.InterruptedException - if any thread interrupted the current thread before or while the current thread was waiting for job execution to finish. The interrupted status of the current thread is cleared when this exception is thrown.
        java.util.concurrent.TimeoutException - if the timeout has run out however the job is not JobStatus.CANCELLED or JobStatus.FINISHED
